name: t16_reliability_gate_v1

observable_quality_channel.definition: Relative observed-view error between calibrated physics lift reprojection and noisy input observation.
observable_quality_channel.normalization: log residual, train-only mean/std, clipped to +/-4 sigma and divided by four

experiments.label: Residual FNO
experiments.purpose: Reference with alpha fixed to one.

experiments.label: Fixed view gate
experiments.purpose: Keep alpha=1 for 5/7-view training conditions and reduce it to 0.6 for three-view OOD.

experiments.label: Learned metadata gate
experiments.purpose: Learn alpha from observable view fraction and declared noise/quality metadata.

experiments.label: Residual FNO + observed residual
experiments.purpose: Control whether the extra observable quality channel helps without a gate.

experiments.label: Learned observable gate
experiments.purpose: Learn alpha from view fraction, metadata, and train-normalized observed-view lift reprojection residual.

interpretation_contract.oracle: Per seed-domain minimum of Residual FNO and Absolute-output FNO; uses truth metrics for audit only.
interpretation_contract.gate_inputs: View fraction, declared quality metadata, and optional observed-view lift reprojection residual.
interpretation_contract.fixed_gate: Alpha equals one for 5/7-view training cells and 0.6 for three-view OOD.
interpretation_contract.learned_gate_initialization: Alpha starts at one and is jointly trained with the FNO correction backbone.

claims_boundary: No gate input uses field truth, clean observation, or held-out-view labels.
The oracle is available only for retrospective synthetic audit and cannot select a model on real data.
The learned gates see only 5/7-view training conditions, so three-view behavior is extrapolation.
Three optimization seeds are a stability screen, not a population-level uncertainty estimate.
The linear synthetic forward model is not the full OERF BOST geometry.
This benchmark remains framewise 3D and does not train a temporal or four-dimensional operator.
